title: Michael and Vin continuation
summary: Vinnie Micciche expressed deep concern over Michael Donovan's lack of actionable marketing opportunities and leads, emphasizing urgent need for speed and content output. Michael Donovan shared background and noted fundamental ICP differences. Vinnie stressed collaboration, clear deliverables, lead generation, and simple, exciting content for 'car guys' and dealer groups, while critiquing Michael's content drafts and prioritization.

decisions.description: Michael Donovan to simplify messaging for car guys.
Michael Donovan to send new social media posts immediately.
Michael Donovan to refine social media post designs for lead generation.
Michael Donovan to follow up with Perry regarding Viconic website.
Vinnie Micciche to provide new content ideas to Michael Donovan for posting.

actionItems.task: Tone down messaging to be less complex and more direct.
Ensure new social media posts are sent out immediately.
Refine social media post designs to be in-your-face and low-funnel.
Circle back with Perry regarding the VCON site.
Create and send new content ideas to Michael Donovan.

contributions.description: Emphasized urgent need for actionable marketing opportunities and leads.
Stressed immediate content output over building a marketing 'engine.'
Shared background on leaving mortgage industry due to fraud incidents.
Explained transition to automotive by working on motorcycles and mechanics.
Expressed desire to attract dealer groups for scalability, citing Catchy BT.
Criticized Michael for not prioritizing urgent tasks given financial structure.
Presented a $100 million idea about being middleware between LLMs and dealers.
Critiqued Michael's draft content for not resonating with 'car guys.'
Proposed refining social posts to be more 'in-your-face' and low-funnel.
Admitted fundamental differences with Vinnie on the Ideal Customer Profile.
Emphasized using AI tools like ChatGPT for speed in content generation.
Stated management priorities are employee, dealer, customer satisfaction, then profit.
Suggested Strawlet posts follow Vinnie's LinkedIn lead to capitalize on audience.
